source: Local simplified MITRE ATT&CK mapping (no external fetch). Used for educational/demo purposes.

T1110.name: Brute Force
T1110.description: Adversaries may use brute force techniques to gain access to accounts when passwords are unknown or when password hashes are obtained.

T1059.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
T1059.description: Adversaries may abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands, scripts, or binaries.

T1190.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
T1190.description: Adversaries may attempt to take advantage of a weakness in an Internet-facing computer or program.

T1087.name: Account Discovery
T1087.description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of accounts on a system or within an environment.

T1046.name: Network Service Discovery
T1046.description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of services running on remote hosts and local network infrastructure devices.

T1021.name: Remote Services
T1021.description: Adversaries may use valid accounts to log into a service that accepts remote connections.

T1071.name: Application Layer Protocol
T1071.description: Adversaries may communicate using application layer protocols to avoid detection by blending in with existing traffic.

T1105.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
T1105.description: Adversaries may transfer tools or other files from an external system into a compromised environment.

T1003.name: OS Credential Dumping
T1003.description: Adversaries may attempt to dump credentials to obtain account login and credential material.

T1055.name: Process Injection
T1055.description: Adversaries may inject code into processes in order to evade process-based defenses as well as possibly elevate privileges.

T1566.name: Phishing
T1566.description: Adversaries may send phishing messages to gain access to victim systems.

T1595.name: Active Scanning
T1595.description: Adversaries may execute active reconnaissance scans to gather information that can be used during targeting.
